2603.25727.title: Back to Basics: Revisiting ASR in the Age of Voice Agents
2603.25727.paper_summary: Automatic speech recognition (ASR) systems have achieved near-human accuracy on curated benchmarks, yet still fail in real-world voice agents under conditions that current evaluations do not systematically cover. Without diagnostic tools that isolate specific failure factors, practitioners cannot anticipate which conditions, in which languages, will cause what degree of degradation. We introduce WildASR, a multilingual (four-language) diagnostic benchmark sourced entirely from real human speech that factorizes ASR robustness along three axes: environmental degradation, demographic shift, and linguistic diversity. Evaluating seven widely used ASR systems, we find severe and uneven performance degradation, and model robustness does not transfer across languages or conditions. Critically, models often hallucinate plausible but unspoken content under partial or degraded inputs, creating concrete safety risks for downstream agent behavior. Our results demonstrate that targeted, factor-isolated evaluation is essential for understanding and improving ASR reliability in production systems. Besides the benchmark itself, we also present three analytical tools that practitioners can use to guide deployment decisions.
2603.25727.paper_summary_zh: 自動語音識別（ASR）系統在經過精心挑選的基準測試中已達到接近人類的準確度，然而在現實世界的語音代理中，仍然在當前評估未系統性涵蓋的條件下失敗。<lb/>缺乏能夠隔離特定失敗因素的診斷工具，從業者無法預測哪些條件、在何種語言中會導致何種程度的性能下降。<lb/>我們介紹了WildASR，這是一個多語言（四種語言）的診斷基準，完全來源於真實的人類語音，並從三個方面對ASR的穩健性進行了分解：環境退化、人口變遷和語言多樣性。<lb/>在評估七個廣泛使用的ASR系統時，我們發現性能下降嚴重且不均，並且模型的穩健性無法在不同語言或條件之間轉移。<lb/>重要的是，模型在部分或退化的輸入下經常會幻想出看似合理但未被表達的內容，這為下游代理行為帶來了具體的安全風險。<lb/>我們的結果表明，針對性、因素隔離的評估對於理解和改善生產系統中ASR的可靠性至關重要。<lb/>除了基準本身，我們還提出了三種分析工具，從業者可以利用這些工具來指導部署決策。
2603.25727.author: Geeyang Tay et.al.
2603.25727.authors: Geeyang Tay, Wentao Ma, Jaewon Lee, Yuzhi Tang, Daniel Lee, Weisu Yin, Dongming Shen, Silin Meng, Yi Zhu, Mu Li, Alex Smola
